channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan skater Kelvin Kiarie explains why he postponed his daughter’s schooling to compete in Benin
item.description: Kenyan skater Kelvin Kiarie recently caught national attention after winning two medals at the International Skating Challenge held in Benin. Despite facing financial struggles and lack of government ...

item.title: Digital Realty and IXPN open new Lagos internet exchange to enhance Nigeria’s network
item.description: Digital Realty and the Internet Exchange Point of Nigeria (IXPN) don open new internet exchange point for Lagos wey go help improve connectivity and reduce cost for network operators. The new facility...

item.title: CAG Reports 41% of Public Institutions Operating at Loss Due to Inefficiency
item.description: The Controller and Auditor General (CAG), Charles Kichere, show that 22 out of 54 public institutions, or 41 percent, dey operate at loss because of unproductive investments, high running costs, and w...

item.title: Environmentalists Intervene to Protect Antarctic Krill Populations
item.description: Environmental activists are increasingly confronting commercial krill fishing in the Southern Ocean, warning of the ecological consequences of overexploitation. Krill, tiny shrimp-like creatures avera...

item.title: Journalists Experience Limited Access During Islamabad US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Hundreds of journalists from around the world gathered in Islamabad for the historic US-Iran talks aimed at ending the Middle East conflict. Pakistani authorities had set up the Jinnah Convention Cent...

item.title: Isaac Mwaura Refuses to Give Up Seat Reserved for CS at Kabarak Funeral
item.description: During a funeral in Kabarak, Nakuru County, government spokesperson Isaac Mwaura caused tension when he refused to vacate a seat reserved for Trade and Industry CS Lee Kinyanjui. Interior CS Kipchumba...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla calls for government monitoring and legal action on excessive private hospital fees
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla on Saturday emphasized the need for stricter government oversight and clearer judicial precedents to address the growing issue of high medical costs in private hospital...

item.title: The Limits of Constant Positivity and Finding Real Joy
item.description: Canadian author Kate Bowler challenges the culture of relentless positivity in her latest book. As a religious historian at Duke University and a cancer survivor, Bowler rejects the idea that gratitud...

item.title: Angélique Kidjo honours her mother with a new rendition of ‘Malaika’ on upcoming album ‘Hope’
item.description: Grammy-winning artist Angélique Kidjo has paid a touching tribute to her late mother through a new rendition of the classic Swahili love song ‘Malaika’. The song will feature in her upcoming album tit...

item.title: Ten Killed and 25 Injured in Bus-Pickup Collision in Katihar, Bihar
item.description: On April 11, 2026, a serious road accident took place near Gerabari, under the jurisdiction of Korha Police Station in Katihar district, Bihar. A bus collided with a pickup van on National Highway 31,...

item.title: Vernon mobile home park residents face indefinite power outage due to unsafe electrical system
item.description: Residents of the Crown Villa mobile home park in Vernon, B.C., are experiencing an indefinite power outage after Technical Safety B.C. deemed the park's electrical system unsafe. The decision to disco...

item.title: CAG urges for better funding to strengthen Tanzania’s embassies and improve economic diplomacy
item.description: The Controller and Auditor General (CAG), Charles Kichere, has urged the Tanzanian government to improve funding for embassies and foreign representative offices so they can effectively promote trade,...

item.title: Penny Wong urges restraint amid escalating Israel–Lebanon conflict and uncertain Strait of Hormuz situation
item.description: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong has called on Israel and Hezbollah to adhere to a fragile ceasefire following a new wave of Israeli strikes on Lebanon, which reportedly killed more than 250 peo...

item.title: Helen Zille Highlights City Infrastructure Issues During Impromptu Pothole Swim
item.description: DA Johannesburg mayoral candidate Helen Zille has explained the story behind the viral video of her swimming in a pothole in Douglasdale. The incident, which she had dubbed a 'free Saturday afternoon ...

item.title: Arsenal struggle against Bournemouth highlights tactical and psychological challenges for Arteta
item.description: Arsenal suffered a 2-1 defeat to Bournemouth, raising concerns about both their tactical approach and psychological readiness in the title race. Manager Mikel Arteta admitted the team made basic error...

item.title: Amnesty International Raises Alarm Over Rising Kidnappings in Northern Nigeria
item.description: Since the start of 2026, Nigeria dey face serious abduction wahala, especially for northern states. Amnesty International don report say over 1100 people don kidnapped between January and April. Most ...

item.title: Nigerian Man's Innovation Wins Top Prize at 2024 Science Fair
item.description: A young Nigerian man named Emeka has emerged victorious at the 2024 Science Fair, securing the top prize for his groundbreaking innovation. The fair, which attracted participants from across the natio...

item.title: Hawthorn responds to Sam Mitchell’s halftime spray to secure a strong win over Bulldogs
item.description: During a tense AFL clash at Adelaide Oval, Hawthorn faced a moment of vulnerability late in the third quarter against the previously unbeaten Western Bulldogs. A sharp halftime spray from coach Sam Mi...

item.title: Michael Jackson’s Sons Attend Berlin Premiere of Father’s Biopic Amid Family Absences
item.description: Michael Jackson’s sons, Prince and Bigi, made a rare public appearance at the Berlin premiere of 'Michael', a biopic telling the story of the King of Pop. They turned heads with outfits paying tribute...

item.title: US and Iran Delegations Hold First Face-to-Face Talks in Islamabad Amid Ceasefire
item.description: Historic direct talks between the United States and Iran are underway in Islamabad, Pakistan, marking the first face-to-face negotiations in 47 years. The discussions come after Pakistan brokered a te...
